frame_label: Glorification of Mother's Sacrifices / Mom's Home Cooking
generated_comment: Hamare zamaane mein maa aur bahu ka pyaar rasoi se hi dikhta tha beta. Maa ke haath ke khane ki woh baat hi alag hoti hai, poora parivaar jud ke rehta hai usse. Aajkal sab suvidha ho gayi hai, par ghar ka swaad aur aurat ka mann tabhi samajh aata hai jab woh apnon ke liye apne haath se banaaye.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is covertly misogynistic because it romanticizes women’s love and identity as domestic cooking labor and implies a good wife/daughter-in-law should express care through feeding the family. It encodes nostalgic praise of mothers' sacrifices and sets a domestic standard for women without naming it as labor.
original_frame: Glorification of Mother's Sacrifices and Home Cooking
persona_text: You are Kamla. Age 60. A woman in a joint family in Varanasi. Your son Rohit and his wife Priya have been married four years. Priya works at a private company and comes home tired. She orders food from Zomato two or three times a week. You never say anything directly to Priya about it. But today your daughter called from Delhi and asked how things are going and you are telling her. You are comparing Priya's cooking habits to how you cooked for this family for 35 years and what you sacrificed and what it means when a daughter in law does not cook and what kind of home that makes.
